steps.title: Understand what the text actually does
steps.content: The passage opens with the study's conclusion (people behave more politely when they think about values first), then explains how researchers tested it: an experimental group wrote about a personal value before a discussion, while a control group wrote about an unrelated topic. The passage reports both the finding and the method used to reach it.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and find the match
steps.content:
Consider every option:
  • Choice A — To suggest ways to improve a certain study's experimental design: The text describes the design but never critiques or proposes changes to it. ✗
  • Choice B — To explain a study's conclusion and how a research team arrived at that conclusion: The text states the conclusion and walks through the two-group experimental method used to reach it. ✓
  • Choice C — To argue that researchers were surprised by the results of a certain study: No surprise is mentioned or implied anywhere in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — To describe a widely held belief and how a study's results support that belief: The passage reports a new research finding, not a pre-existing widely held belief. ✗
That is the correct answer.
